nodes.name: QuantumProblemDefinition
nodes.description: Initializes the quantum annealing problem. It computes simulation parameters like the minimum and maximum coupling strengths (u) based on the graph and register coordinates, and prepares the initial state for the solver.

inputs.name: graph
inputs.constraints: A graph structure representing the optimization problem.

inputs.name: coordinates_layout
inputs.constraints: Spatial layout of qubits in the register.

inputs.name: num_sol
inputs.constraints: Number of desired solutions.

inputs.name: display_info
inputs.constraints: Flag to control informational display.

outputs.name: initial_register
outputs.constraints: The initialized quantum register.

outputs.name: simulation_parameters
outputs.constraints: Parameters for the adiabatic sequence.

outputs.name: permutation_list
outputs.constraints: List of permutations for the quantum loop.

outputs.name: backend_flags
outputs.constraints: Flags to select the simulation backend (qutip, mps, sv).

outputs.name: num_solutions_passthrough
outputs.constraints: The number of solutions, passed through to the final processing stage.

nodes.type_signature: (graph: ProblemGraph, coordinates_layout: RegisterCoordinates, num_sol: Integer, display_info: Boolean) -> tuple[QuantumRegister, Dict, List[Permutation], Dict, Integer]
nodes.conceptual_summary: SpatialGraphParameterInitializer. Initializes the parameter space for a system simulation by analyzing the geometric embedding of a relational graph. It calculates the extremal values of an interaction function defined over the graph's edges based on the spatial coordinates of their vertices. This process establishes the boundary conditions and initial state for a subsequent solver or iterative process.. Applications: Molecular Dynamics: Calculating the minimum and maximum electrostatic potentials between atoms in a protein based on their 3D positions to initialize a folding simulation., Urban Planning: Determining optimal operational zones in a city by analyzing a road network graph and the geographic locations of facilities to set constraints for a logistics solver., Finite Element Analysis: Initializing the boundary conditions for a stress simulation on a mechanical part by computing maximum force constraints from the mesh geometry., Neuroscience: Setting up a neural activity simulation by calculating the range of effective synaptic strengths based on the physical proximity of neurons in a connectome.

nodes.name: ExtractGraphProperties
nodes.description: Extracts node weights and a corresponding list of node identifiers from the input graph.

inputs.name: graph
inputs.constraints: Graph with 'weight' attribute on nodes.

outputs.name: original_weight_array
outputs.constraints: 1D array of node weights.

outputs.name: node_ids
outputs.constraints: List of node identifiers.

nodes.name: CreateInitialRegister
nodes.description: Builds the quantum register by assigning physical coordinates to each node (qubit) from the graph.

inputs.name: node_ids
inputs.constraints: List of node identifiers.

inputs.name: coordinates_layout
inputs.constraints: Mapping from node ID to coordinate.

outputs.name: initial_register
outputs.constraints: pulser_analog.Register object.

outputs.name: register_coordinates
outputs.constraints: Mapping from node ID to coordinate for nodes in the graph.

nodes.name: OptimizeRegisterLayout
nodes.description: Finds and applies a permutation to the register and weights to minimize the interaction bandwidth, aiming to improve simulation efficiency.

inputs.name: initial_register
inputs.constraints: Unoptimized pulser_analog.Register object.

inputs.name: original_weight_array
inputs.constraints: 1D array of node weights, ordered according to initial_register.

outputs.name: optimized_register
outputs.constraints: Permuted pulser_analog.Register object.

outputs.name: permuted_weights
outputs.constraints: List of weights, reordered according to the optimized layout.

outputs.name: permutation_list
outputs.constraints: The permutation indices used to reorder the register.

nodes.type_signature: (initial_register: Register, original_weight_array: np.ndarray) -> tuple[Register, List[float], List[int]]

nodes.name: CalculateHamiltonianScale
nodes.description: Determines the global energy scale for the Hamiltonian by calculating the interaction strength for the shortest non-connected qubit pair, which defines the Rydberg blockade radius.

inputs.name: register_coordinates
inputs.constraints: Mapping from node ID to coordinate.

inputs.name: graph
inputs.constraints: The problem graph defining connectivity.

outputs.name: max_interaction_strength
outputs.constraints: The calculated 'u_max' value.

nodes.name: FormulateDetuningProblem
nodes.description: Encodes the problem's objective function into the hardware by normalizing weights and mapping them to a local detuning field (DMM) and global detuning strength.

inputs.name: permuted_weights
inputs.constraints: Reordered list of node weights.

inputs.name: max_interaction_strength
inputs.constraints: The 'u_max' value for scaling.

outputs.name: inverted_normalized_weights
outputs.constraints: 1D array of (1 - normalized_weights).

outputs.name: detuning_spread
outputs.constraints: Calculated spread for the DMM.

outputs.name: base_hamiltonian_params
outputs.constraints: Dictionary with evolution duration, amplitude, and detuning maximums.

nodes.type_signature: (permuted_weights: List[float], max_interaction_strength: float) -> tuple[np.ndarray, float, Dict]

nodes.name: ExecuteQuantumAnnealing
nodes.description: Performs the quantum evolution by running a simulation. It selects an appropriate backend (State Vector or Matrix Product State) based on the number of atoms and constructs the final DMM map.

inputs.name: base_hamiltonian_params
inputs.constraints: Core parameters for the quantum evolution.

inputs.name: inverted_normalized_weights
inputs.constraints: Weights used to create the DMM map.

inputs.name: detuning_spread
inputs.constraints: Global detuning for the DMM.

inputs.name: initial_register
inputs.constraints: Required for small-scale simulations.

inputs.name: optimized_register
inputs.constraints: Required for large-scale simulations.

inputs.name: permutation_list
inputs.constraints: Used to correctly map weights in large-scale simulations.

outputs.name: measurement_counts
outputs.constraints: A dictionary mapping final quantum states (as bitstrings) to their occurrence counts.

nodes.type_signature: (base_hamiltonian_params: Dict, inverted_normalized_weights: np.ndarray, detuning_spread: float, initial_register: Register, optimized_register: Register, permutation_list: List[int]) -> Dict

nodes.name: DecodeSolutions
nodes.description: Processes the raw measurement counts from the simulation, sorting them by probability and converting the most frequent results into valid Maximum-Weight Independent Set (MWIS) solutions.

inputs.name: measurement_counts
inputs.constraints: Mapping of quantum states to their counts.

inputs.name: initial_register
inputs.constraints: Used to map qubit indices back to node IDs.

inputs.name: num_solutions_to_find
inputs.constraints: The desired number of MWIS solutions.

outputs.name: mwis_solutions
outputs.constraints: A list of the top MWIS solutions found.

nodes.type_signature: (measurement_counts: Dict, initial_register: Register, num_solutions_to_find: int) -> List[List[int]]

nodes.name: AdiabaticQuantumSampler
nodes.description: Executes the core quantum simulation loop. It evolves the quantum register through an adiabatic sequence to find low-energy states of the problem Hamiltonian, effectively sampling from the solution distribution.

inputs.name: initial_register
inputs.constraints: The initialized quantum register.

inputs.name: simulation_parameters
inputs.constraints: Parameters for the adiabatic sequence.

inputs.name: permutation_list
inputs.constraints: List of permutations for the quantum loop.

inputs.name: backend_flags
inputs.constraints: Flags to select the simulation backend (qutip, mps, sv).

outputs.name: measurement_counts
outputs.constraints: A distribution of measurement outcomes from the simulation.

outputs.name: final_register
outputs.constraints: The final state of the quantum register after evolution.

nodes.type_signature: (initial_register: QuantumRegister, simulation_parameters: Dict, permutation_list: List[Permutation], backend_flags: Dict) -> tuple[CountDistribution, QuantumRegister]
nodes.conceptual_summary: HeuristicMinimumSampler. Evolves a high-dimensional state vector through a scheduled series of transformations. The process is designed to find low-value configurations of a complex objective function by gradually deforming an initial, simple landscape into a target problem landscape, thereby guiding the state towards a minimum. It returns a probability distribution of the most likely low-value final states.. Applications: Protein Folding: Simulating an annealing process to find the minimum energy conformation (the native state) of a polypeptide chain., Logistics Optimization: Finding near-optimal solutions to combinatorial problems like the Traveling Salesperson Problem by encoding the cost function into the system's energy landscape., Financial Modeling: Discovering optimal asset allocations in a portfolio that minimize a complex risk-and-return objective function., VLSI Design: Determining the physical placement of logic gates on a chip to minimize wire length and signal interference, treating the layout as the state and cost as the energy.

nodes.name: build_adiabatic_sequence
nodes.description: Constructs the adiabatic pulse sequence based on simulation parameters and register geometry. This includes defining the amplitude and detuning waveforms for the global Rydberg channel and configuring the local detuning map.

inputs.name: register
inputs.constraints: Defines the geometry of the quantum processor.

inputs.name: parameters
inputs.constraints: Contains keys for sequence properties like 'duration', 'amplitude_maximum', 'detuning_maximum', and 'dmm_map'.

outputs.name: adiabatic_sequence
outputs.constraints: A full description of the pulses to be run on the device.

nodes.name: initialize_simulation_backend
nodes.description: Initializes and configures the appropriate quantum simulation backend (QuTip, State Vector, or MPS) for the given pulse sequence, based on runtime flags. This involves setting up backend-specific configurations like time steps, observables, and log levels.

inputs.name: adiabatic_sequence
inputs.constraints: isinstance(x, (list, tuple))

inputs.name: backend_flags
inputs.constraints: Contains boolean flags like 'run_qutip', 'run_sv', 'run_emu_mps'.

outputs.name: configured_simulator
outputs.constraints: The initialized simulator object ready for execution.

nodes.type_signature: (adiabatic_sequence: Sequence, backend_flags: Dict) -> Union[QutipEmulator, SVBackend, MPSBackend]

nodes.name: run_quantum_simulation
nodes.description: Executes the simulation on the configured backend to produce raw results, which may include final state vectors or bitstring measurements over time.

inputs.name: configured_simulator
inputs.constraints: x is not None

outputs.name: raw_simulation_results
outputs.constraints: Backend-specific results object containing state vectors or measurement data.

nodes.name: process_simulation_results
nodes.description: Extracts final measurement counts from the raw simulation results by sampling the final state. For the MPS backend, it also applies an inverse permutation to the sampled bitstrings to align them with the original qubit order.

inputs.name: raw_simulation_results
inputs.constraints: x is not None

inputs.name: permutation_list
inputs.constraints: Required only for specific backends (e.g., MPS) that require result reordering.

inputs.name: backend_flags
inputs.constraints: Used to determine the correct result extraction and processing logic for the backend that was run.

outputs.name: measurement_counts
outputs.constraints: A distribution of measured final states.

nodes.type_signature: (raw_simulation_results: SimulationResults, permutation_list: List[Permutation], backend_flags: Dict) -> CountDistribution

nodes.name: SolutionExtraction
nodes.description: Processes the raw results from the quantum sampler. It interprets the measurement count distribution to extract the top solutions for the optimization problem.

inputs.name: measurement_counts
inputs.constraints: A distribution of measurement outcomes from the simulation.

inputs.name: final_register
inputs.constraints: The final state of the quantum register.

inputs.name: num_solutions
inputs.constraints: The number of top solutions to extract.

outputs.name: solutions
outputs.constraints: The final, processed solutions to the problem.

nodes.type_signature: (measurement_counts: CountDistribution, final_register: QuantumRegister, num_solutions: Integer) -> List[Solution]
nodes.conceptual_summary: MostProbableStateDecoder. Filters a discrete probability distribution to identify the 'K' most frequently observed states. It then decodes these high-frequency states from a raw format into a structured representation, effectively reducing a large outcome space to a small set of the most likely results.. Applications: Genomics: Identifying the most common genetic variants in a population from sequencing count data., Natural Language Processing: Extracting the most likely candidate words from a language model's output probability distribution for use in beam search decoding., Failure Analysis: Pinpointing the most frequent error codes from system logs to prioritize investigation of system failure modes., Market Research: Determining the most popular product choices from a large set of customer transaction data.
